commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
This PR updates gateway trusted-proxy and protocol documentation to describe device-less Control UI WebSocket scope clearing, the `x-openclaw-scopes` upgrade-header cap, and missing-scope troubleshooting.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as a runtime reproduction; this is a docs-only PR. The behavior it documents is source-verifiable from current gateway code and the existing Control UI auth tests.
commands.review_followup: A narrow automated docs repair can reconcile the stale branch and preserve current protocol wording while adding the missing WebSocket scope-cap guidance.

target.title: docs: clarify trusted-proxy Control UI scope behavior
target.body:
Fixes #80063.

## Summary
- Clarify that trusted-proxy Control UI WebSocket sessions can connect without device identity, but device-less sessions have their requested scopes cleared.
- Document the `x-openclaw-scopes` WebSocket upgrade header as a scope cap (intersection), and add a troubleshooting entry for the "connected but missing scope" failure mode.
- Add a short note in the gateway protocol docs about scope clearing for unbound device-less sessions.

## Real behavior proof
- Docs-only change; no runtime behavior changes.
- Verified against existing gateway tests and code paths:
  - `src/gateway/server.auth.control-ui.suite.ts` includes:
    - `clears trusted-proxy control ui scopes without device identity`
    - `bounds trusted-proxy control ui scopes to proxy-declared scope header`
  - `src/gateway/server/ws-connection/message-handler.ts` implements the `x-openclaw-scopes` intersection cap for Control UI upgrade requests.
- Not tested on a live gateway instance (docs-only alignment to existing behavior).
target.author: nielskaspers
